pr.title: Fix binaryornot Python 3 crash on binary reads
pr.body:
<!-- Keep this PR as draft until it is ready for review. -->

<!-- AI/LLM agents: be concise and specific. Do not check the box below. -->

- [ ] A human has tested these changes.

---

## Why

`binaryornot==0.4.4` raises `NameError: name 'unicode' is not defined` under Python 3 when checking some non-ASCII binary files. Although the direct `pyproject.toml` pin had already been removed, the lockfiles still resolved `binaryornot` to 0.4.4 because `openhands-aci==0.3.3` required `binaryornot>=0.4.4,<0.5.0`.

Fixes #14136.

## Summary

- Add an explicit `binaryornot>=0.5.0,<1` dependency constraint.
- Remove the unused `openhands-aci` dependency that forced `binaryornot<0.5.0` in the lockfiles.
- Regenerate root/enterprise Poetry locks and `uv.lock`, and add a regression test for non-ASCII binary file detection.

## Issue Number

Fixes #14136

## How to Test

- `poetry run pytest tests/unit/test_binaryornot_dependency.py -q`
- `poetry run pre-commit run --config ./dev_config/python/.pre-commit-config.yaml`

## Video/Screenshots

N/A — dependency/runtime crash fix with automated test coverage.

## Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Breaking change
- [ ] Docs / chore

## Notes

I verified there are no repository imports of `openhands_aci`; the action execution server file editor path uses `openhands-tools`, which only requires `binaryornot>=0.4.4` and works with the updated `binaryornot` lock.
